name: Matrix
summary: Represents an arbitrarily sized matrix of double-precision floating point numbers. If you are working with a 4x4 matrix, then you may want to use the Transform class instead.

constructors.name: Matrix(Transform)
constructors.summary: Initializes a new instance of the matrix based on a 4x4 matrix Transform.

params.name: xform
params.doc: A 4x4 matrix to copy from.

constructors.name: Matrix(Int32, Int32)
constructors.summary: Initializes a new instance of the matrix.

params.name: rowCount
params.doc: A positive integer, or 0, for the number of rows.

params.name: columnCount
params.doc: A positive integer, or 0, for the number of columns.

throws.when: If either rowCount, or columnCount or both are negative.

methods.name: BackSolve
methods.signature: public double[] BackSolve(double zeroTolerance, double[] b)
methods.summary: Solves M*x=b where M is upper triangular with a unit diagonal and b is a column of values.

params.name: zeroTolerance
params.doc: (>=0.0) used to test for "zero" values in b in under determined systems of equations.

params.name: b
params.doc: The values in B[RowCount],...,B[B.Length-1] are tested to make sure they are within "zeroTolerance".

returns.doc: Array of length ColumnCount on success. null on error.

methods.name: BackSolvePoints
methods.signature: public Point3d[] BackSolvePoints(double zeroTolerance, Point3d[] b)
methods.summary: Solves M*x=b where M is upper triangular with a unit diagonal and b is a column of 3d points.

params.name: zeroTolerance
params.doc: (>=0.0) used to test for "zero" values in b in under determined systems of equations.

params.name: b
params.doc: The values in B[RowCount],...,B[B.Length-1] are tested to make sure they are "zero".

returns.doc: Array of length ColumnCount on success. null on error.

methods.name: Dispose()
methods.summary: Actively reclaims unmanaged resources that this instance uses.

methods.name: Dispose(Boolean)
methods.summary: For derived class implementers. This method is called with argument true when class user calls Dispose(), while with argument false when the Garbage Collector invokes the finalizer, or Finalize() method.You must reclaim all used unmanaged resources in both cases, and can use this chance to call Dispose on disposable fields if the argument is true.Also, you must call the base virtual method within your overriding method.

params.name: disposing
params.doc: true if the call comes from the Dispose() method; false if it comes from the Garbage Collector finalizer.

methods.name: Duplicate
methods.summary: Create a duplicate of this matrix.

returns.doc: An exact duplicate of this matrix.

methods.name: Finalize
methods.summary: Passively reclaims unmanaged resources when the class user did not explicitly call Dispose().

methods.name: GetHashCode
methods.summary: Gets the hash code for this matrix. The hash code will change when the matrix changes so you cannot change matrices while they are stored in hash tables.

methods.name: Invert
methods.summary: Modifies this matrix to become its own inverse. Matrix might be non-invertible (singular) and the return value will be false.

params.name: zeroTolerance
params.doc: The admitted tolerance for 0.

returns.doc: true if operation succeeded; otherwise false.

methods.name: RowReduce(Double, Point3d[], Double)
methods.signature: public int RowReduce(double zeroTolerance, Point3d[] b, out double pivot)
methods.summary: Row reduces a matrix as the first step in solving M*X=b where b is a column of 3d points.
methods.remarks: The matrix itself is row reduced so that the result is an upper triangular matrix with 1's on the diagonal.

params.name: zeroTolerance
params.doc: (>=0.0) zero tolerance for pivot test. If the absolute value of a pivot is <= zero_tolerance, then the pivot is assumed to be zero.

params.name: b
params.doc: An array of RowCount 3d points that is row reduced with the matrix.

params.name: pivot
params.doc: The value of the smallest pivot is returned here.

methods.name: RowReduce(Double, Double, Double)
methods.signature: public int RowReduce(double zeroTolerance, out double determinant, out double pivot)
methods.summary: Row reduces a matrix to calculate rank and determinant.
methods.remarks: The matrix itself is row reduced so that the result is an upper triangular matrix with 1's on the diagonal.

params.name: zeroTolerance
params.doc: (>=0.0) zero tolerance for pivot test. If a the absolute value of a pivot is <= zeroTolerance, then the pivot is assumed to be zero.

params.name: determinant
params.doc: value of determinant is returned here.

params.name: pivot
params.doc: value of the smallest pivot is returned here.

methods.name: RowReduce(Double, Double[], Double)
methods.signature: public int RowReduce(double zeroTolerance, double[] b, out double pivot)
methods.summary: Row reduces a matrix as the first step in solving M*X=b where b is a column of values.
methods.remarks: The matrix itself is row reduced so that the result is an upper triangular matrix with 1's on the diagonal.

params.name: zeroTolerance
params.doc: (>=0.0) zero tolerance for pivot test. If the absolute value of a pivot is <= zero_tolerance, then the pivot is assumed to be zero.

params.name: b
params.doc: an array of RowCount values that is row reduced with the matrix.

params.name: pivot
params.doc: the value of the smallest pivot is returned here.

methods.name: Scale
methods.summary: Modifies the current matrix by multiplying its values by a number.

params.name: s
params.doc: A scale factor.

methods.name: SetDiagonal
methods.summary: Sets diagonal value and zeros off all non-diagonal values.

params.name: d
params.doc: The new diagonal value.

methods.name: SwapColumns
methods.signature: public bool SwapColumns(int columnA, int columnB)

params.name: columnA
params.doc: A first column.

params.name: columnB
params.doc: Another column.

returns.doc: true if operation succeeded; otherwise false.

methods.name: SwapRows
methods.signature: public bool SwapRows(int rowA, int rowB)

params.name: rowA
params.doc: A first row.

params.name: rowB
params.doc: Another row.

returns.doc: true if operation succeeded; otherwise false.

methods.name: Transpose
methods.summary: Modifies this matrix to be its transpose. This is like swapping rows with columns.http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transpose

returns.doc: true if operation succeeded; otherwise false.

methods.name: Zero
methods.summary: Sets all values inside the matrix to zero.

methods.name: Addition
methods.signature: public static Matrix operator +(Matrix a, Matrix b)
methods.summary: Adds two matrices and returns a new sum matrix.

params.name: a
params.doc: A first matrix to use in calculation.

params.name: b
params.doc: Another matrix to use in calculation.

throws.when: When the two matrices are not the same size.

methods.name: Multiply
methods.signature: public static Matrix operator *(Matrix a, Matrix b)
methods.summary: Multiplies two matrices and returns a new product matrix.

params.name: a
params.doc: A first matrix to use in calculation.

params.name: b
params.doc: Another matrix to use in calculation.

properties.name: IsColumnOrthogonal
properties.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the matrix is column orthogonal.

properties.name: IsColumnOrthoNormal
properties.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the matrix is column orthonormal.

properties.name: IsRowOrthogonal
properties.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the matrix is row orthogonal.

properties.name: IsRowOrthoNormal
properties.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the matrix is row orthonormal.

properties.name: IsSquare
properties.summary: Gets a value indicating whether this matrix has the same number of rows and columns. 0x0 matrices are not considered square.

properties.name: IsValid
properties.summary: Gets a value indicating whether this matrix is valid.

properties.name: Item
properties.summary: Gets or sets the matrix value at the given row and column indices.
